commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-20 (RE-KILL) — n=264, total=-4.292 SOL, drop_top3=-6.937 SOL. Was incorrectly pre-resolved on 05-19 (likely my prior kill commit 7f3b3d5a didn't process or got reverted by a subsequent redeploy). Strategy continued trading for ~3 more days and accumulated another -2.5 SOL net + -2.8 SOL drop_top3 loss. Confirms the original 05-17 verdict: TP=150/SL=25 on the bot-excluded universe is structurally negative-EV and gets worse with more samples. Operator playbook update: when killing, verify the strategy actually disappears from strategies.json on the next sync — don't assume the delete processed.

KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. HIT-KILL. The peak-analysis-EV hypothesis (TP=75 as the EV maximum vs TP=100) was falsified. Peak-analysis suggested +3.11% EV at TP=75 vs +2.73% at TP=100 — but live data on n=100+ trades shows TP=75 loses money on the bot-excluded universe. Why peak-analysis misled: max_relret_0_300 is a LOOK-AHEAD signal computed across the full T+0..T+300 window — it counts a TP-eligible peak even if the price spent most of the window dumping. The realized exit (with gap penalty + slippage) on a brief +75% touch in an otherwise-dumping window is meaningfully worse than the look-ahead suggests. Lesson: peak-analysis CDFs are DIAGNOSTIC ONLY. Trust the sim counterfactual (which actually simulates the exit) over peak-analysis EV when they disagree.

KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. HIT-KILL. TP=75 + trailing TP=10% on the bot-excluded universe. The trailing TP modification did NOT rescue the TP=75 base — losses still accumulated. Confirms two patterns simultaneously: (1) TP=75 is wrong for this universe (per v27-bot-excl-tp75 result), AND (2) trailing TP doesn't compensate when the underlying TP threshold is too low. Mechanism: trailing TP only fires when the +75% threshold is crossed; if the +75% threshold itself is selecting for the wrong subset (brief spikes that retrace), trailing the exit doesn't help — the population was wrong from the start. Lesson: trailing TP layers on TOP of a TP threshold — it can't fix a bad threshold.

KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. TP=75 + tighter SL=20 was the most aggressive of the v27 variants. The SL tightening hypothesis (-24% net realized loss instead of -36% per SL fire) didn't compensate for the TP=75 problem. SL=20 fired MORE often (more trades crossed -20% than -30%) AND each SL fire was on a trade that would have hit the wrong TP threshold anyway. Compound effect: more SL fires + same broken TP target = worst of the three v27 variants. Lesson on combined changes: when stacking two changes (TP shift + SL shift), if EITHER change is wrong-direction, the combination is worse than either alone. Test single-lever changes before stacking.

KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=393: total positive but drop_top3=-0.505 SOL. The strategy's edge was entirely concentrated in the top 3 lottery trades; remove them and it loses money. FRONT-RUNNER FALLOUT: this was our most promising strategy as of 05-12 (n=121, drop_top3=+1.636, score=100 on the Promotion Readiness scorecard). At n=243 the drop_top3 had eroded to +0.51, and at n=393 it crossed to negative. The lottery tickets stayed roughly fixed in absolute magnitude as n grew, so their PROPORTIONAL contribution shrank — but the marginal trade was net negative, dragging the drop_top3 across zero. CRITICAL LESSON: drop_top3 must be re-checked AS N GROWS — passing the gate at n=100 doesn't mean the strategy is robust. The promotion-bar n≥100 is a MINIMUM, not a sufficient condition. Strategies should be re-evaluated against the SOL bar continuously, especially the drop_top3 check, which is the most outlier-sensitive metric. The 'pure exclusion' universe (top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60s) appears to be insufficient on its own — the v25-bot-excl-climbing variant (with C5 climbing overlay) is the configuration that's still winning. Future iterations: use the bot-excluded universe ONLY paired with a positive-feature overlay (currently climbing_t30_t45 is the proven one).
